reason: Both correctly identify alert hygiene as the priority and give a similar audit-and-tune process. B is more detailed and adds two good points A lacks (symptom-vs-cause alerting, explicit weekly review cadence), but it also recommends "follow-the-sun" as a stopgap, which directly conflicts with the key insight that follow-the-sun isn't viable with only six engineers — a misleading suggestion in an answer meant to steer the team away from restructuring the rotation. A is slightly less detailed but stays fully correct throughout and correctly concludes the existing 1-in-6 rotation is fine once paging noise drops, without floating an infeasible schedule fix.
